facts.statement: A vaccine trains the immune system to recognize a pathogen without causing the disease.
questions: What does a vaccine do to the immune system?
How do vaccines help the body recognize pathogens?
Do vaccines cause disease while training immunity?

facts.statement: The first vaccine was developed by Edward Jenner in 1796.
questions: When was the first vaccine developed?
What year did Edward Jenner create the first vaccine?

facts.statement: Edward Jenner used cowpox to protect against smallpox.
questions: What did Edward Jenner use to protect against smallpox?
How did Edward Jenner's vaccine work?
What disease did cowpox vaccination prevent?

facts.statement: Vaccines work by exposing the body to an antigen, prompting it to produce antibodies and memory cells.
questions: How do vaccines work in the body?
What do vaccines expose the body to?
What does the body produce in response to vaccine antigens?

facts.statement: Widespread vaccination led to the global eradication of smallpox, declared in 1980.
questions: What led to the global eradication of smallpox?
In what year was smallpox eradication declared?

facts.statement: Herd immunity occurs when enough of a population is immune to slow disease spread.
questions: What happens when enough people in a population are immune to a disease?
